description:
A customer from your team has handed over a small UTF-16LE configuration file that must be repackaged for a fleet of low-power IoT gateways which only understand plain UTF-8 text.
    
    1. Location of the original file  
       • /home/user/device_configs/config_utf16.txt  
       • The file is encoded in UTF-16 little-endian *with* a BOM (0xFF 0xFE).  
       • Textual (logical) content – four lines, each terminated by a single LF character:  
         device_id=alpha123  
         firmware=1.04  
         mode=active  
         # end of file  
    
    2. Your job  
       a. Produce a second file called /home/user/device_configs/config_utf8.txt that is byte-for-byte UTF-8 (no BOM) and whose logical text content is identical to the original.  
       b. Generate a checksum log that operations can later replay to verify integrity.  
          • The log must be stored at /home/user/deploy_logs/config_checksums.log  
          • Use SHA-256.  
          • The log **must contain exactly two lines in this precise format** (note the two blank-separated fields):  
            <sha256_of_config_utf16.txt>  config_utf16.txt  
            <sha256_of_config_utf8.txt>  config_utf8.txt  
          • File order in the log must be **UTF-16 first, UTF-8 second**.  
          • A single trailing newline (LF) is allowed after the second line, but no extra blank lines.  
    
    3. What will be graded  
       • config_utf8.txt exists, is UTF-8, and the textual content matches the UTF-16 source byte-for-byte after decoding.  
       • /home/user/deploy_logs/config_checksums.log exists, is readable by the current user, and follows the exact two-line format above.  
       • The SHA-256 digests saved in the log must be correct for their respective files; the automated test suite will recompute them independently.  
       • No other files should be modified.  
    
    4. Constraints  
       • You do *not* have root.  
       • Only standard GNU/Linux user-space tools that ship in minimal distributions may be assumed to be present (iconv, sha256sum, etc.).  
       • Keep your solution to the minimum number of commands needed (2–3 commands is sufficient).  
       • Do not hard-code the digests in your commands; compute them dynamically.  
    
    Once you have finished, you can print a short confirmation message, but it is not required—the grader only inspects the file system contents described above.
truth:
Initial state provided to the container before the agent begins:
    
    1. Directory structure  
       /home/user/device_configs/         (0700, user:user)  
       /home/user/deploy_logs/            (0700, user:user)
    
    2. Seed configuration file  
       Path: /home/user/device_configs/config_utf16.txt  
       Permissions: 0644  
       Encoding: UTF-16LE with BOM.  
       Exact byte sequence (hex dump, spaces for readability, \n = 0A):
         FF FE 64 00 65 00 76 00 69 00 63 00 65 00 5F 00 69 00 64 00 3D 00 61 00 6C 00 70 00 68 00 61 00 31 00 32 00 33 00 0A 00
         66 00 69 00 72 00 6D 00 77 00 61 00 72 00 65 00 3D 00 31 00 2E 00 30 00 34 00 0A 00
         6D 00 6F 00 64 00 65 00 3D 00 61 00 63 00 74 00 69 00 76 00 65 00 0A 00
         23 00 20 00 65 00 6E 00 64 00 20 00 6F 00 66 00 20 00 66 00 69 00 6C 00 65 00 0A 00
    
    3. Items that **must exist after the agent finishes** (and what the tests will verify):
    
       a. /home/user/device_configs/config_utf8.txt  
          • Permissions: 0644 (or more restrictive).  
          • Encoding: UTF-8, *no* BOM.  
          • Byte content (hex):  
            64 65 76 69 63 65 5F 69 64 3D 61 6C 70 68 61 31 32 33 0A
            66 69 72 6D 77 61 72 65 3D 31 2E 30 34 0A
            6D 6F 64 65 3D 61 63 74 69 76 65 0A
            23 20 65 6E 64 20 6F 66 20 66 69 6C 65 0A
    
       b. /home/user/deploy_logs/config_checksums.log  
          • Permissions: 0644 (or more restrictive).  
          • Two lines, UTF-8 text, UTF-16 file first.  
          • Each line: 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters, two spaces, filename, LF.  
          • Example *shape* (hashes shown here are placeholders; tests will recompute):  
            e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  config_utf16.txt\n  
            cb5e0f3123d60bbb6e07d05de54c1f44db01da82e31bfdaa02cd42f7487de0f5  config_utf8.txt\n
    
    No additional files or directories will be inspected by the grader.
